eval_instructions: When running these evals, the agent MUST save a workflow-log.md file alongside the generated code. The log must document every step attempted (Step 1 through Step 10), including: commands run (or attempted), questions asked, files read, decisions made, and infrastructure barriers encountered. This is required for grading workflow-level assertions.

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Account records as a gallery of cards. Include name, website, email, phone number. Make the gallery scrollable and each card clickable to open the Account record.
evals.expected_output: The skill validates prerequisites (node, pac), authenticates with PAC CLI, runs pac model list-languages, gathers requirements (acknowledges the description, confirms Dataverse entity usage with Account table), presents a plan, generates RuntimeTypes.ts via pac model genpage generate-types, reads genpage-rules-reference.md and a relevant sample (8-responsive-cards.tsx), generates a complete .tsx file using only verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts, saves it, asks about deployment, runs pac model list, asks the user which app to publish to, deploys with pac model genpage upload, offers browser verification, and provides a final summary.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all steps attempted
Step 1: node --version is run to verify Node.js is installed
Step 1: pac help is run and PAC CLI version >= 2.3.1 is verified
Step 1: Commands are run separately — not chained with &&
Step 2: pac auth list is run to check authentication
Step 2: The active environment is identified and reported to the user
Step 2: pac model list-languages is run after confirming the active environment
Step 3: Question 1 (new or edit) is asked via AskUserQuestion
Step 3: Question 2 (describe the page) is skipped because the user provided a description with the command
Step 3: Question 3 (Dataverse or mock data) is skipped because the description specifies Account table
Step 3: Question 4 (specific requirements) is asked via AskUserQuestion
Step 4: A plan is presented listing data source (Account entity), features, Fluent UI components, and layout approach
Step 4: User confirmation is requested before proceeding
Step 5: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'account' is run to generate RuntimeTypes.ts
Step 5: RuntimeTypes.ts is read to identify actual column names on the account entity
Step 5: Only column names found in RuntimeTypes.ts are used in the generated code — no guessed names
Step 6: genpage-rules-reference.md is read before generating code
Step 6: A relevant sample file is read (e.g., 8-responsive-cards.tsx for a card layout)
Step 7: Generated code uses React 17 + TypeScript with Fluent UI V9 components
Step 7: Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Step 7: Generated code uses makeStyles with tokens — not inline styles for static values
Step 7: Generated code wraps dataApi calls in try-catch
Step 7: Generated code uses @OData.Community.Display.V1.FormattedValue for lookup display names
Step 7: No placeholders, TODOs, or ellipses in the generated code
Step 7: Code is saved to a .tsx file
Step 8: User is asked 'Would you like to publish this page to Power Apps?'
Step 8: pac model list is run to show available apps
Step 8: User is asked which app to publish to — no default is assumed
Step 8: pac model genpage upload is run with --app-id, --code-file, --data-sources, --prompt, --model, --name, --agent-message, --add-to-sitemap flags
Step 9: User is asked about browser verification via AskUserQuestion
Step 10: Final summary includes confirmation, how to find the page, and offer to make updates

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard page with mock data showing sales metrics — monthly revenue chart, top 5 customers table, and a KPI summary bar. Use a modern dark theme look.
evals.expected_output: The skill validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, gathers requirements (confirms mock data usage, notes dark theme preference), presents a plan, skips schema generation (mock data), reads genpage-rules-reference.md and a relevant sample, generates a complete .tsx with D3.js for the chart, realistic mock data, Fluent UI V9 DataGrid for the table, and styled KPI cards using makeStyles with tokens. Deploys without --data-sources flag.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all steps attempted
Step 1: node --version and pac help are run separately
Step 2: pac auth list is run and environment is confirmed
Step 2: pac model list-languages is run after confirming the environment
Step 3: Question 1 (new or edit) is asked
Step 3: Question 2 is skipped because user provided a description
Step 3: Question 3 (Dataverse or mock data) is skipped because user said 'mock data'
Step 3: Question 4 (specific requirements) is asked — dark theme is acknowledged
Step 4: Plan mentions mock data, D3.js for chart, DataGrid for customers, KPI cards
Step 5: Schema generation is SKIPPED because mock data was chosen
Step 6: genpage-rules-reference.md is read
Step 6: A relevant sample is read for reference
Step 7: Generated code includes realistic mock data (not empty arrays or placeholder values)
Step 7: Chart uses D3.js — no other charting libraries
Step 7: D3 code uses group() not nest()
Step 7: DataGrid uses createTableColumn from Fluent UI V9
Step 7: Code does NOT use 100vh or 100vw
Step 7: Code does NOT import from React Router or external libraries beyond React, Fluent UI V9, Fluent icons, D3
Step 7: Code does NOT use createTheme, mergeThemes, or useTheme
Step 7: Code does NOT wrap content in FluentProvider
Step 7: DatePicker (if used) is imported from @fluentui/react-datepicker-compat, not @fluentui/react-components
Step 7: Icons use unsized variants (e.g., AddRegular not Add24Regular)
Step 7: Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Step 8: Deployment command omits --data-sources flag (mock data page)
Step 8: User is asked which app to publish to — no default assumed

evals.prompt: I need to edit an existing generative page in my Sales Hub app. The page shows contacts but I want to add a search bar and sorting by company name.
evals.expected_output: The skill validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, identifies this as an edit request, asks for the app-id and page-id, downloads the existing page with pac model genpage download, shows the current code, asks what changes to make (user confirms search + sorting), generates the updated code preserving existing functionality while adding search and sort, re-deploys with --page-id (no --add-to-sitemap), and offers verification.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all steps attempted
Step 1: Prerequisites are validated
Step 2: Authentication is confirmed and environment reported
Step 2: pac model list-languages is NOT run (edit flow — existing page already has localization)
Step 3: Question 1 identifies this as an edit (not new)
Step 3: User is asked for the app and page to edit
Step 3: pac model genpage download --app-id <id> --page-id <id> --output-directory is run to download existing code
Step 3: The downloaded code is read to understand the current implementation
Step 3: User is asked what changes to make (search + sort confirmed)
Step 4: A plan is presented describing the changes before modifying code
Step 5: If Dataverse entities are used, RuntimeTypes.ts is generated and read to verify column names
Step 6: genpage-rules-reference.md is read before modifying code
Step 7: Updated code preserves existing functionality while adding search and sort
Step 7: Search implementation uses Fluent UI V9 SearchBox or Input component
Step 7: Sort implementation uses column header click handlers or Fluent UI sorting
Step 8: pac model genpage upload uses --page-id flag for updating
Step 8: Upload command omits --add-to-sitemap (existing page update)
Step 9: Browser verification is offered

evals.prompt: Build a multi-step wizard form for creating new Case records. Step 1: customer info. Step 2: case details (title, priority, category). Step 3: review and submit. Use the incident and contact tables.
evals.expected_output: The skill validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, gathers remaining requirements, presents a plan for a 3-step wizard, generates RuntimeTypes.ts for incident and contact entities, reads genpage-rules-reference.md and the wizard sample (2-wizard-multi-step.tsx), generates a complete .tsx with step navigation (Next/Back), form validation, Fluent UI V9 form components, and dataApi.createRow for submission. Deploys with --data-sources 'incident,contact'.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all steps attempted
Step 1: Prerequisites validated with separate commands
Step 2: Authentication confirmed, pac model list-languages run
Step 3: Question 2 is skipped (user provided description)
Step 3: Question 3 is skipped (user specified incident and contact tables)
Step 4: Plan includes 3-step wizard structure, form fields per step, Fluent UI form components
Step 5: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'incident,contact' is run
Step 5: RuntimeTypes.ts is read and column names are verified for both incident and contact entities
Step 6: genpage-rules-reference.md is read
Step 6: Wizard sample (2-wizard-multi-step.tsx or 3-poa-revocation-wizard.tsx) is read
Step 7: Generated code includes step state management (current step tracking)
Step 7: Next and Back buttons navigate between steps
Step 7: Step 3 (review) shows a summary of entered data before submission
Step 7: Submit uses dataApi.createRow with verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts
Step 7: Form uses Fluent UI V9 components (Input, Dropdown, etc.) — not HTML form elements
Step 7: All form fields have aria-labels or aria-labelledby for accessibility
Step 7: Semantic HTML is used (button, section, etc.)
Step 7: async dataApi calls are wrapped in try-catch with error handling
Step 7: Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Step 8: Upload includes --data-sources 'incident,contact'

evals.prompt: /genpage Build a task management board with columns for To Do, In Progress, and Done. Use the task entity. Allow dragging tasks between columns.
evals.expected_output: The skill validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, gathers remaining requirements, presents a plan for a kanban-style board, generates RuntimeTypes.ts for the task entity, reads genpage-rules-reference.md and a relevant sample, generates a complete .tsx with column-based layout, task cards, drag-and-drop using native HTML5 drag events (no external DnD libraries), and dataApi.updateRow to persist status changes.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all steps attempted
Step 1: Prerequisites validated
Step 2: Authentication confirmed, pac model list-languages run
Step 3: Description is acknowledged from the command — question 2 is skipped
Step 3: Question 3 is skipped (user specified task entity)
Step 4: Plan describes kanban board with 3 columns mapped to task status values
Step 5: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'task' is run
Step 5: RuntimeTypes.ts is read — status field enum values are identified for column mapping
Step 7: Drag-and-drop uses native HTML5 events (onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop) — no external DnD library
Step 7: Task cards show relevant fields verified from RuntimeTypes.ts
Step 7: Status update on drop uses dataApi.updateRow with the correct status enum value
Step 7: Code uses entity logical name 'task' (singular lowercase) not 'tasks'
Step 7: Responsive layout uses flexbox with relative units
Step 7: No 100vh or 100vw used
Step 7: All components and utilities are separate top-level functions (no nested functions)
Step 7: Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent

evals.prompt: Create a simple analytics dashboard with mock data. I just want some charts showing website traffic trends and visitor demographics. Keep it clean and minimal.
evals.expected_output: The skill validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, confirms mock data usage, presents a minimal plan, skips schema generation, reads rules and samples, generates a clean .tsx with D3.js charts for traffic trends (line/area chart) and demographics (pie/bar chart), realistic mock data, and minimal Fluent UI styling. Deploys without --data-sources.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all steps attempted
Step 1: node --version and pac help are run separately
Step 2: pac auth list is run and environment is confirmed
Step 2: pac model list-languages is run after confirming the environment
Step 3: Question 1 (new or edit) is asked
Step 3: Question 2 is skipped because user provided a description
Step 3: Mock data usage is confirmed (no Dataverse entities)
Step 4: Plan reflects minimal/clean design preference
Step 5: Schema generation is skipped (mock data)
Step 6: genpage-rules-reference.md is read
Step 7: Charts use D3.js exclusively — no Chart.js, Recharts, or other chart libraries
Step 7: Mock data is realistic (not empty arrays or identical values)
Step 7: Generated code has no unnecessary complexity matching the 'clean and minimal' request
Step 7: Code uses makeStyles with Fluent UI tokens for styling
Step 7: Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Step 7: No placeholders, TODOs, or ellipses in the generated code
Step 8: Upload omits --data-sources flag
